subject: Re: Reenstra
body:
ask him what the c++ were going for ,,, two years ago
if you signed up early...

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

> Reenstra told me that there are no used C++s
> available and that he just was  
> outbid for the last available used C+ by $0.5 MM
> (Reenstra claims he offered  
> $5.8 MM and was outbid with an offer of $6.3 MM
> which the seller accepted for  
> the C+).
>  
> Reenstra volunteered (I did not ask) that he could
> probably get you a  decent 
> place on line for a new  C++; but he said that he
> would not try to  do it 
> unless I told him that you were sure you wanted one.
>  I told him that  I did not 
> know if you did (figuring that if you told me to do
> so, I could ask  Reenstra 
> to find out how early a delivery date he could get
> you for a new C++),  Please 
> let me know if you wish me to ask Reenstra to find
> out the earliest  delivery 
> date Reenstra could get you to see if it is sooner
> than the delivery  date 
> that Larry spoke to me about.
>  
> Reenstra also said that a new C++ could cost well in
> excess of $10  MM.
>  
> Darren
>

body_html:
ask him what the c++ were going for ,,, two years ago
if you signed up early...

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

Reenstra told me that there are no used C++s
available and that he just was
outbid for the last available used C+ by $0.5 MM
(Reenstra claims he offered
$5.8 MM and was outbid with an offer of $6.3 MM
which the seller accepted for
the C+).

Reenstra volunteered (I did not ask) that he could
probably get you a decent
place on line for a new C++; but he said that he
would not try to do it
unless I told him that you were sure you wanted one.
I told him that I did not
know if you did (figuring that if you told me to do
so, I could ask Reenstra
to find out how early a delivery date he could get
you for a new C++), Please
let me know if you wish me to ask Reenstra to find
out the earliest delivery
date Reenstra could get you to see if it is sooner
than the delivery date
that Larry spoke to me about.

Reenstra also said that a new C++ could cost well in
excess of $10 MM.

Darren
